findings.title: Missing test: getProvider local-to-lightweight fallback path
findings.description: The getProvider method has a fallback from local to lightweight when local is undefined (line 180). However, there is no test that exercises this specific fallback path. The existing test 'falls back to lightweight when local is not available for classification' tests the resolveTier logic which already routes to 'lightweight' when no local provider exists, so the ?? fallback in getProvider is never reached. If an override forces tier='local' but no local provider is configured, the ?? fallback would be the only safety net, and it is untested.

findings.title: Incomplete error path coverage for ModelRouter
findings.description: The error handling section has only one test case (provider throws Error). Missing error scenarios: (1) provider throws non-Error (string, undefined), (2) provider returns a rejected promise with a typed error, (3) error from standard provider (current test only fails the lightweight provider). These paths exercise different code branches since generateText and generateStructured both delegate to different providers.

findings.title: Missing test: classifyTask with ambiguous/multi-keyword prompts
findings.description: classifyTask is a pure function with keyword-priority-based classification, but no test verifies behavior when a prompt contains keywords from multiple categories. For example, 'Classify and summarize the entities' contains keywords for classification, summarization, and extraction. The implementation has a specific priority order (classification > extraction > summarization > generation), which is a critical business decision that should be explicitly tested as a boundary condition.

findings.title: Performance benchmark memory assertions are vacuously true
findings.description: The memory budget tests assert that creating a ModelRouter + DeduplicationTracker uses less than 100MB (lightweight) or 500MB (full). These objects consist of a few counters and function references -- they use kilobytes at most. The assertions will never fail because the actual overhead is ~0.001-0.1MB, making the thresholds 1000x-500000x too generous. Additionally, RSS measurements are inherently noisy (GC timing, V8 heap growth) and can show negative overhead. These tests provide false confidence about memory budgets without actually enforcing meaningful constraints.
findings.suggestion:
Either tighten the bounds to realistic values (e.g., <1MB for lightweight, <5MB for full) or replace RSS-based tests with a design-level assertion that verifies no large buffers or caches are created. Alternatively, document that these are smoke tests for the overall system memory budget (including the test runner itself) and adjust the description accordingly. If the intent is to guard against accidentally adding large data structures, consider testing object size directly:

expect(overheadMB).toBeLessThan(1) // ModelRouter + tracker should be kilobytes

findings.title: Interface compliance test is trivially weak
findings.description: This test checks that ModelRouter has generateText and generateStructured methods by verifying their typeof is 'function'. This is already guaranteed by TypeScript's 'implements LlmProvider' clause at compile time. The test name says 'integration with ContextMerger' but does not actually test integration with ContextMerger -- it is a type-level assertion dressed as an integration test.

positive_observations: Test doubles for LlmProvider use proper '// Test Double rationale:' comment convention, correctly identifying LLM providers as external API boundaries
No vi.fn(), vi.mock(), jest.fn(), or jest.mock() violations detected -- all test doubles are hand-crafted interface implementations
classifyTask (Functional Core) is tested as a pure function with direct instantiation, no mocks needed -- correct testing approach
DeduplicationTracker tests include excellent boundary conditions: zero comparisons, all duplicates, all unique, and reset behavior
dedup-stats.test.ts includes a real integration test using sharp image processing with computePHash, testing real image similarity detection rather than mocked values
Routing stats tests verify state accumulation and reset behavior thoroughly
Test structure separates pure function tests from routing behavior tests cleanly
